title: Kotilingeshwara Temple
tagline: Kotilingeshwara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Kotilingeshwara Temple (Kannada: ಕೋಟಿಲಿಂಗೇಶ್ವರ) a Hindu temple in the village of Kammasandra in Kolar district, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Kotilingeshwara Temple (Kannada: ಕೋಟಿಲಿಂಗೇಶ್ವರ) a Hindu temple in the village of Kammasandra in Kolar district, Karnataka, India. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva. The temple has one of the largest Shivalingams in the world.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Guttahalli, Kolar Gold Fields
topPlaces.description: Guttahalli is a small village in the Kolar Gold Fields Taluk of Kolar district in Karnataka, India. It is situated about 4 kilometers from Kolar Gold Fields, forming the outskirts of the city.

topPlaces.name: Adampalli
topPlaces.description: Adampalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Bangarapet taluk of Kolar district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Bethamangala
topPlaces.description: Bethamangala is a small town in the Kolar Gold Field taluk of Kolar district in the state of Karnataka, India. The town is centred around a large man-made lake created by damming the Palar river.

topPlaces.name: Kolar Gold Fields
topPlaces.description: Kolar Gold Fields (K.G.F.) is a mining region in K.G.F. taluk (township), Kolar district, Karnataka, India. It is headquartered in Robertsonpet, where employees of Bharat Gold Mines Limited (BGML) and BEML Limited (formerly Bharat Earth Movers Limited) and their families live.

topPlaces.name: Coromandel, KGF
topPlaces.description: Coromandel is a mining area in Kolar Gold Fields(KGF), Karnataka, India. Where major mining units are located.

topPlaces.name: King George Hall, Kolar Gold Fields
topPlaces.description: King George Hall is a colonial-era civic building located in Robertsonpet, part of the Kolar Gold Fields (KGF) township in Karnataka, India. The hall was constructed to commemorate the Delhi Durbar of 1911, which marked the formal coronation of George V as Emperor of India and the announcement of shifting the imperial capital from Calcutta to Delhi.

image.alt: King George Hall, Kolar Gold Fields

topPlaces.name: Robertsonpet
topPlaces.description: Robertsonpet is a township in the city of Kolar Gold Fields in India. The township was planned and built to accommodate the increasing population of the city of Kolar Gold Fields.

topPlaces.name: Palar Nagar
topPlaces.description: Palar Nagar is a village situated in Kolar Gold Fields of Kolar district of Karnataka State in India. It is near Aalamaram.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kotilingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kotilingeshwara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kotilingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (68 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (78 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kotilingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kotilingeshwara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kotilingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kotilingeshwara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kotilingeshwara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Guttahalli, Kolar Gold Fields, Adampalli, Bethamangala, Kolar Gold Fields, Coromandel, KGF.
